map.0:
A simple plaque, bearing the name of the deceased.

(Everyone has this by default.)
map.6: The four-pointed star is universally recognised as the symbol of Saradomin.
map.4: An elaborate font. As water collects in the bowl, it symbolises new life and hope.
map.12: The ultimate marker for a grave, this statue evokes intimations of mortality in all who are faced with it.
map.13: The dwarves of Keldagrim are said to be the world's greatest sculptors, and a gravestone of dwarven design is an unparallelled work of art.
map.8: Favoured by humans of a mystical or druidic inclination.
map.9: Followers of Bandos are now scarce, but this symbol is still used by those who remember that violent god.
map.11: Adventurers often request this symbol, although its origin and meaning have become obscure in the centuries since the god wars.
map.10: Armadyl's love of winged creatures is represented by this elegant symbol. Little else is remembered of him.
map.1: A dignified little flag flutters in the breeze.
map.7: This serpentine sigil represents the ruthless Zamorak.
map.5: A monolithic memorial stone bearing a selection of emblems.

map.0:
Welcome to Free Chat|You are free to chat to other users simply by typing. This is fun and you will make lots of new friends, however, it's important to be careful and stay safe. To do so:

-Never give out personal information such as your real name, age, phone number or school.

-Never share your password with anyone except your parents. Someone else might use your password to pretend to be you and do something that may get you into trouble.

-If someone says or does something that makes you uncomfortable, or if someone asks you for personal information, tell your parents and use the 'Report Abuse' button to report them to Jagex.

Quick Chat: Navigation Basics|The Quick Chat interface can be triggered either by hitting [ENTER] on a new line in the chat window or by left-clicking your user name at the bottom of the chat window.

You can navigate the menus by left-clicking on options, by pressing one of shortcut keys listed next to them or by scrolling with [PAGE UP] and [PAGE DOWN] and selecting with [ENTER].

Choosing something with a next to it will open up another set of options in the next column. Anything without a is what you will actually say.
map.1:
Quick Chat: Saying Things|Usually, selecting a phrase will automatically make your character say it, however, you will find that some of the things that you can say have a '...' in them. This indicates that you must choose something to complete this phrase. Selecting one of these phrases will either bring up a list of appropriate choices or will prompt you to type in the name of the object you want to talk about.

If you wish to avoid scrolling down a large list simply press the first letter of the option you are looking for and it will jump to the first item on the list beginning with that letter.

You can repeat the last thing you have said using Quick Chat at any time by pressing [F11].
map.3:
Quick Chat: Context Sensitive Chat|The last item on the Quick Chat menu (always the [X] shortcut) is context sensitive, This means that it will change depending on what you are doing. If you are playing an activity, it will offer chat relevant to the activity you are playing; otherwise, it will refer to the skill you most recently gained experience in.

You can jump to the context sensitive menu at any time by hitting [F10], even if the Quick Chat interface is not currently open.
map.2: Quick Chat: Futher Navigation|You may return to a previous section of the menu by either <col=8080FF>left-clicking<col=FFFFFF> on it, selecting it on the trail at the top of the interface or by pressing <col=FFFF00>[BACK SPACE]<col=FFFFFF> to move back one menu layer or <col=FFFF00>[HOME]<col=FFFFFF> to return to the beginning.
map.6: Quick Chat: Ready To Go|You are now ready to get started with the quick-chat system. You can access a  reference for all the shortcut keys or view this guide again at any time by <col=8080FF>left-clicking<col=FFFFFF> the ? icon in the top left hand corner of the quick-chat interface.
map.4:
Quick Chat: Auto-responses|If you see a message in your chat log with a next to it, this means that you can auto-respond to it. Right-clicking one of these messages and selecting auto-respond will bring up a list of suitable responses from which to choose. This will save you lots of time!

Hitting [F9] will bring up a list of auto-responses to the last thing in your chat log.
map.5:
Quick Chat: Clan Chat and Friends Chat|You can still message your friends and use Clan Chat with the Quick Chat system.

To talk to a friend simply right-click their name and select �Quick Message� from the menu.

To talk in Clan Chat you can either right-click your user name and select �Clan Quick Chat� or you can hit [/] before pressing [ENTER] to call up the menu.

map.319: I could do with some freshly cooked anchovies for a salad I'm planning.
map.1919: My glassware got damaged when I moved here, so I need some new beer glasses.
map.526: My plans for today require some sets of bones, the normal-sized sort you get from goblins.
map.882: I'm short of ammunition, so I could do with some bronze arrows. That way I can shoot at goblins through my window.
map.1205: I need a few bronze daggers to keep the goblins at bay.
map.1351: The head fell off my hatchet when I was chopping wood last week, so I need some more bronze hachets.
map.1422: I'd like some maces made of bronze.
map.1139: I could do with some medium-sized helmets. I'd like bronze ones.
map.1277: My weapons collection isn't all it used to be. Some short swords made of bronze would be much appreciated.
map.1917: Strange as it may seem, I want a lot of beer!
map.753: I've found a use for cadava berries.
map.2140: I'm running short of food, so I'd like some cooked chickens.
map.2142: I'm a bit hungry. I'd like some cooked meat, not chicken or any sort of bird. And definitely not camel or rabbit either!
map.436: I need a few lumps of copper ore.
map.1944: I'm going to make an omelette, so I'll need some eggs.
map.314: I need a handful of feathers to stick in my beard.
map.1947: I'd like a bit of grain.
map.1420: Some iron maces would be useful.
map.440: A few lumps of iron ore would be nice.
map.1761: I'll need some clay that's been softened so I can craft it.
map.1059: It's a bit nippy in this house, and my hands are cold. I need some leather gloves.
map.1511: This house is a bit cold, so I could do with some normal logs to burn.
map.1775: Some chunks of molten glass would be the ideal patch for my cracked window.
map.1942: I need some potatoes, if you'd be so kind.
map.2134: I hear pet cats are getting popular these days. I'd like some raw rat in case I get one.
map.1436: I'd like to study the rune essence that the wizards have been talking about recently, so I need a few pieces.
map.315: I could do with some freshly cooked shrimps for a salad I'm planning.
map.950: My undergarments are getting a bit worn out. I'll be needing some sheets of silk to patch them.
map.1741: I'll be needing a few pieces of soft leather.
map.438: I need a few lumps of tin ore.
map.1759: I saw an interesting bed in Karamja called a 'hammock'. It seemed to be made out of string, and I'd like some balls of wool so I can make my own.
map.1777: My shortbow's string is getting a bit worn out, so could you fetch me some bow strings?
map.2309: I don't have a decent larder here, so I can't store food very well. Now I'm out of loaves of bread.
map.39: I need a few bronze arrowheads to complete some arrows I was making.
map.864: I'd like some bronze knives to throw at goblins.
map.1337: Could you fetch me some big warhammers made of bronze?
map.1794: I need some lengths of bronze wire to repair something.
map.53: I want to make some arrows, so I'll need some headless arrows that have the feathers attached.
map.1941: My roof is leaking, so I need some swamp paste to fix it.
map.40: I need some iron arrowheads to put on the arrows I was making.
map.863: I could do with some iron throwing knives to keep the goblins away from my house.
map.1335: I'd like some chunky iron warhammers.
map.1167: The hail can be very heavy here, so I'd like a few leather cowls.
map.1933: I'm out of flour, so I could do with a few pots of that.
map.1789: Strange as this may seem, I need some unfired pie dishes.
map.1787: Believe it or not, I need a few unfired clay pots.
map.1061: My footwear is getting a bit worn out, so I need a few pairs of leather boots.

map.319: Use a small fishing net to get anchovies from the sea south of here. Then cook them on a fire or range.
map.1919: If you get some seaweed and a bucket of sand, you'll be able to mix them at a furnace to make molten glass. Then use a glassblowing pipe to make beer glasses. Most of what you need can be found on Entrana.
map.526: You'll find bones easily enough if you fight creatures.
map.882: You can make them by chopping a log into arrowshafts and adding feathers to them, then smithing a bronze bar into arrowheads to complete the arrows.
map.2349: Mine some copper ore and tin ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of bronze.
map.1205: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make daggers.
map.1351: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make hatchets.
map.1422: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make maces.
map.1139: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make helmets.
map.1237: If you kill some of those pesky goblins, you're bound to get some bronze spears.
map.1277: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make short swords.
map.1917: There's a pub not far from here that will sell you plenty of that. Go west to Port Sarim.
map.753: Just look around the woods south-west of Varrock.
map.2140: You could try killing some chickens, then cooking their meat.
map.2142: Just cook some beef, rat or bear.
map.436: It should be easy enough for you to mine some copper ore.
map.1739: If you slaughter some cows, you'll get cowhides easily enough.
map.1944: Eggs are usually found where chickens are farmed.
map.314: The cheapest way to get feathers is to kill chickens.
map.1947: There's a field full of it north of here.
map.2351: Mine some iron ore. The furnace in Lumbridge can smelt them into bars of iron.
map.1420: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make maces.
map.440: It should be easy enough for you to mine some iron ore.
map.1761: If you mine some clay, you can use containers of water on it to soften it.
map.1059: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to sew the gloves yourself. Buy a needle & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid.
map.1511: I suggest you take a hatchet and chop down some standard trees.
map.1775: Use a bucket of sand with some soda ash on a furnace.
map.1942: There's a field of those north of Lumbridge.
map.2134: You should find some big rats south-east of here in the swamp.
map.1436: Ask the Archmage in the tower south of here to teleport you to the Rune Essence mine. Then you can mine me some pieces.
map.315: Use a small fishing net to get shrimps from the sea south of here. Then cook them on a fire or range.
map.950: There's a man in Al Kharid who sells it very cheaply. If you ever take any to Ardougne you'll get a good profit.
map.1741: There's a tanner in Al Kharid who will turn cowhides into leather.
map.438: You shouldn't have any trouble mining tin ore.
map.1759: You can buy shears from the general store east of here. Shear some sheep, then spin the wool into balls on the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle.
map.1777: If you pick some flax, you can use the spinning wheel in Lumbridge Castle to spin it into bowstrings.
map.2309: Mix some flour and water to make bread dough, then bake it on a cooking range.
map.39: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make arrowtips.
map.864: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make knives.
map.1337: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make warhammers.
map.1794: Try smelting some copper and tin ore to make bronze. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make wire.
map.53: Use a knife to chop some logs into arrowshafts. Then add a feather to each arrowshaft.
map.1941: Swamp tar is found south-east of here. Add it to a pot of flour and cook it on an open fire to make the paste.
map.40: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make arrowtips.
map.863: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make knives.
map.1335: Try smelting some iron. Then hammer the bronze on an anvil to make warhammers.
map.1167: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the cowls yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.
map.1933: There's a windmill north of here. You can use it to grind grain into flour. Then use an empty pot to collect the flour.
map.1789: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pie dishes. Just don't bake them.
map.1787: Mine some clay. Then use a container full of water to soften it. In the Barbarian Village you'll be able to form this into pots. Just don't bake them.
map.1061: If you get the tanner in Al Kharid to turn some cowhides into leather, you'll be able to make the boots yourself. Buy needles & thread from the crafting shop in Al Kharid if you need any.

map.456: Father Aereck in Lumbridge is sure to reward you if you take a note to him for me.
map.216: Could you please take a message to the High Priest of Entrana for me?
map.647: Reldo, the librarian in Varrock Palace, wrote to me recently about something. I need you to take my reply to him.
map.604: Thurgo the dwarf is from an ancient tribe. I've found some of its history that he was asking about, and I need you to deliver the information to him.
map.640: I hear my old friend Fr Lawrence in Varrock is drinking a bit too much. I'd like to get a letter to him about it.
map.801: I've written a letter to my old friend Abbot Langley that I'd like you to deliver.
map.746: I'd like to get a note up to the Oracle on Ice Mountain.
map.2255: Well, this is rather embarrassing, but I think there's some kind of monster in my house. Could you go upstairs and get rid of it, please?

map.456: Just head eastwards to Lumbridge. He'll be in the church.
map.216: There are some monks in Port Sarim, west of here, who will take you there so long as you're not carrying any weapons or armour.
map.647: The library is on the ground floor of the Palace in Varrock.
map.604: Go west to Port Sarim, then southwards to some cliffs. He lives on the beach there.
map.640: Varrock is a fairly long way north-east of here. The church is at the eastern side of the town, near the Palace.
map.801: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you find his Monastery. He'll be downstairs.
map.746: Walk northwards, past Draynor Manor. When you reach the Barbarian Village, go north-west until you see the Ice Mountain. It'll be at the top.
map.2255: I think it's somewhere upstairs. It kept me awake all last night.

map.1604: Aberrant Spectres are fetid, vile ghosts. The very smell of them will paralyse and harm you. A nosepeg will help ignore their stink.
map.1615: Abyssal Demons are nasty creatures to fight. They aren't really part of this realm, and are able to move very quickly to trap their prey.
map.9172: Aquanites rely on their lures for both offensive and defensive purposes. Whilst their hides are resistant to slashing attacks, a well timed cut is capable of severing the lure.
map.4381: Ankou are undead skeletal ghosts. Their blows can be powerful but they have limited defences, so killing them quickly is often the best way to protect yourself.
map.6232: Aviansies are bird-like creatures found in the icy dungeons of the north. Melee weapons can't reach them, so use Magic or Ranged attacks.
map.196: Bandits band together for protection against enemies such as yourself.
map.1616: Basilisks, like Cockatrice, have a gaze which will paralyse and harm their prey. You'll need a Mirror Shield to protect you.
map.1612: Banshees use a piercing scream to shock their enemies, you'll need some Earmuffs to protect yourself from them.
map.1618: Bloodveld are strange demonic creatures, their long rasping tongue has magical properties so it pierces straight through physical defences.
map.3707: Brine Rats can be found in caves that are near the sea. They are hairless, bad-tempered and generally unfriendly.
map.106: Bears are tough creatures and fierce fighters, watch out for their powerful claws.
map.412: Bats are rarely found on the ground, so you'll have to fight them while they're airborne, which won't be easy for melee.
map.1475: Birds aren't the most intelligent of creatures, but watch out for their sharp stabbing beaks.
map.84: Powerful weapons known as demonbane weapons can be used to quickly defeat even powerful demons like the Black Demon with greater ease.
map.54: Black Dragons are the strongest dragons and very fierce, watch out for their fiery breath.
map.178: Black Knights like using black armour and weapons, this makes them susceptible to magic attacks.
map.55: Like all dragons, Blue Dragons have thick scales and a powerful fiery breath. Stabbing weapons and heavy bolts are best suited to piercing their defences.
map.1590: Bronze Dragons are the weakest of the metallic dragons, their bronze scales are far thicker than normal bronze armour.
map.4397: Catablepon are mythical, cow like, magical creatures. Beware their weakening glare.
map.1832: Cave Bugs are like Cave Crawlers, except smaller and easier to squish, though they still have a fondness for plants.
map.1600: Cave Crawlers are small and fast, often hiding in ambush. Avoid their barbed tongue or you'll get poisoned.
map.4355: Cave Horrors can be found under Mos Le'Harmless. You will need a Witchwood Icon to fight them effectively.
map.1831: Cave Slimes are the lesser cousins of Jellies, though don't be fooled they can still be dangerous as they're often poisonous.
map.1620: Cockatrice, like Basilisks, have a gaze which will paralyse and harm their prey. You'll need a Mirror Shield to protect you.
map.81: Cows are bigger than you, so they'll often hit fairly hard but are usually fairly slow to react.
map.1648: Crawling Hands are undead severed hands, fast and dexterous they claw their victims.
map.181: Chaos Druids are druids who've turned to evil. Some of them have spells that can harm you, or even teleport you back to them if you flee.
map.1265: Crabs can be found all across Gielinor. They don't hit very hard but they can take a while to kill.
map.1993: Crocodiles are large reptiles which live near water. You'll want to have a stabbing weapon handy for puncturing their thick scaly hides.
map.2783: Dark Beasts are large, dog-like predators. Their massively muscled bodies protect them from crushing weapons.
map.1624: Dust Devils use clouds of dust, sand, ash, and whatever else they can inhale to blind and disorientate their victims. Make sure to wear a facemask when fighting them.
map.1338: Dagannoth are large sea dwelling creatures which are very aggressive. You'll often find them in caves near sea water.
map.192: Dark Warriors wear protective armour. If you want to fight them with melee, try crush-based attacks. Or try magic instead.
map.99: Dogs are much like Wolves, they are pack creatures which will hunt in groups.
map.118: Dwarves are a small but tough race of miners, often using pickaxes to pierce their opponents armour.
map.124: Earth Warriors are a kind of earth elemental, grind them to dust with blunt weapons.
map.1183: Elves are quick, agile, and vicious fighters which often favour bows and polearms.
map.1582: Like other giants, Fire Giants have little to no magical ability, and they're particularly weak to water spells.
map.4389: Fleshcrawlers are scavengers and will eat you - and anyone else, given the chance.
map.2850: Fever Spiders are giant spiders that carry the deadly Spider Fever. If you don't want to catch it I suggest you wear Slayer Gloves to fight them.
map.11633: Frost dragons reside beneath the island of Grimstone. They have thick scales and powerful icy breath. Dragonfire protection is a must.
map.103: Ghosts are undead so magic is your best bet against them, there is even a spell specially for fighting the undead.
map.1610: Gargoyles are winged creatures of stone. You'll need to fight them to near death before breaking them apart with a Rock Hammer.
map.1218: Ghouls aren't undead but they are stronger and tougher than they look. However they're also very cowardly and will run if they're losing a fight.
map.4262: Goblins are mostly just annoying, but they can be vicious. Watch out for the spears they sometimes carry.
map.4418: Goraks are extremely aggressive creatures. They have been imprisoned on an alternative plane, which is only accesible by using the fairyrings. Be extremely careful, their touch drains health as well as skills!
map.83: Demons are particularly weak to water spells. Though Greater Demons are not the strongest type of demon they are still dangerous.
map.941: Like all dragons, Green Dragons have thick scales and a powerful fiery breath. Unless you can stay out of their reach, bringing dragonfire protection is a must.
map.49: Hellhounds are a cross between Dogs and Demons. They are dangerous with a fierce bite, but have all of the same weaknesses as most demons.
map.117: Hill Giants often wield large weapons, learn to recognise what kind of weapon it is and act accordingly.
map.122: Hobgoblins carrying spears are often trained warriors. If you want less of a challenge, fight the ones that are unarmed.
map.3153: Harpie Bug Swarms are pesky critters that are hard to hit. You need a lit bug lantern to distract them with its hypnotic light.
map.1643: Infernal Mages are dangerous spell users, beware of their magic spells and go properly prepared.
map.111: Like other giants, Ice Giants have little to no magical ability, and they're particularly weak to fire spells.
map.125: Ice Warriors are a kind of ice elemental, shatter them with blunt weapons or melt them with fire.
map.7716: Icefiends are beings of ice and freezing rock, they're quick and agile so you'll want to be careful when getting close to them.
map.1591: Iron Dragons are some of the weaker metallic dragons, their iron scales are far thicker than normal iron armour.
map.1637: Jellies are nasty cube-like gelatinous creatures which absorb everything they come across into themselves. Their magical acids bypass physical defences.
map.4352: Jungle Horrors can be found all over Mos Le'Harmless. They are strong and aggressive, so watch out!
map.1153: Kalphite are large insects which live in great hives under the desert sands.
map.3202: Killerwatts store huge amounts of energy in their bodies, which is released if they are touched. You'll need to wear heavily insulated boots to counter this shocking effect.
map.1608: Kurask are large brutal creatures with very thick hides. You'll need a Leaf-Tipped Spear or Battle-axe, Broad Arrows, or a Magic Dart to harm it.
map.82: Demons are weak to magical attacks. Though Lesser Demons are relatively weak they are still dangerous.
map.2803: Lizards are large reptiles with tough skin. Those found in the desert will need you to douse them with freezing water to finish them off after a tough battle.
map.127: Magic Axes have a mind of their own. You may find magic or slash-based melee attacks are slightly more effective against them.
map.114: Mogres are a type of aquatic Ogre that is often mistaken for a giant mudskipper. You have to force them out of the water with a fishing explosive.
map.3346: Mutated Zygomites are hard to destroy. They regenerate quickly so you will need to finish them with fungicide.
map.4404: Minotaurs are large manlike creatures but you'll want to be careful of their horns.
map.5363: Mithril dragons are more vulnerable to magic and to stab-based melee attacks than to anything else.
map.132: Monkeys are tricky creatures, they are agile and fairly fast. Learn to anticipate their movements.
map.112: Like other giants, Moss Giants have little to no magical ability, and they're particularly weak to fire spells.
map.1613: Nechryael are demons of decay which summon small winged beings to help them fight their victims. Their minions can bypass protective prayers so be sure to wear thick armour.
map.852: Ogres are brutal creatures, favouring large blunt maces and clubs, they often attack without warning.
map.126: Otherworldly Beings are ethereal beings making them weak to magical attack.
map.183: Pirates can be found near the sea. They like to collect coins among other things.
map.1633: Pyrefiends are beings of fire and molten rock, they're quick and agile but weak to water spells.
map.47: Rats are your everyday pest, but they can get fairly big. Watch out for their sharp piercing teeth.
map.53: Red Dragons are very powerful, stronger than most dragons, watch out for their fiery breath.
map.187: Rogues are found in lawless areas, such as the deep Wilderness. They can be very protective of their loot.
map.1631: Rockslugs are strange stoney slugs. You'll need to fight them to near death before finishing them off with Salt.
map.5253: Scabarites are insectoid creatures, found beyond the Kharidian deserts. Make sure not to fall into one of their pitfall traps or you'll be quickly overwhelmed.
map.107: Scorpions are almost always poisonous, their hard carapace makes them resistant to crushing and stabbing attacks.
map.3939: Sea Snakes are long and slithery with a venomous bite. The larger ones are more poisonous, so keep an eye on your health.
map.3068: Skeletal Wyverns are extremely dangerous and they are hard to hit with arrows as they slip right through. To stand a good chance of surviving you'll need some elemental shielding from its icy breath.
map.1248: Shades are undead so magic is your best bet against them. You can find Shades at Mort'ton.
map.158: Shadow Warriors are dark and mysterious. They wear spectral plate armour, so crush weapons and magic will give them trouble.
map.6106: Skeletons are undead so magic is your best bet against them, there is even a spell specially for fighting the undead.
map.6221: Spiritual Creatures can be found in the icy caverns near Trollheim, supporting the cause of their chosen god. Wearing equipment aligned with each of the gods can help you avoid undesired conflict.
map.61: Spiders are often poisonous, and many varieties are camouflaged too.
map.1592: Steel Dragons are dangerous metallic dragons, their steel scales are far thicker than normal steel armour.
map.4527: Suqahs can only be found on the mystical Lunar Isle. They are capable of melee and magic attacks and often drop hide, teeth and herbs!
map.1623: Turoth are large vicious creatures with thick hides. You'll need a Leaf-Tipped Spear or Sword, Broad Arrows, or a Magic Dart to harm them.
map.1935: Trolls regenerate damage quickly and have some level of magical defences, but this is easily bypassed by their weakness to fire.
map.2591: Tzhaar reside in their cave under the Karamja volcano. Beware as they can call to their fellows for aid.
map.3531: Vampyres are extremely powerful beings. An underground group known as the Myreque have been searching for tools to defeat them, which they may be willing to share if you can find them.
map.5361: Waterfiends are creatures of water, which live under the Baxtorian Lake. Their watery form is well defended against slashing and piercing weapons, so use something blunt.
map.6006: Werewolves are feral creatures, they are strong and tough with sharp claws and teeth.
map.95: Wolves are pack animals, so you'll always find them in groups. Watch out for their bite, it can be nasty.
map.7823: Wall Beasts are really much larger creatures but you'll only see their arms. You'll want something sharp on your head to stop them grabbing you.
map.6285: Warped Creatures can supposedly be found within a mysterious dungeon on the eastern edge of the Poison Waste. Be aware that to defeat them, you'll need to purify them in some way.
map.73: Zombies are undead so magic is your best bet against them, there is even a spell specially for fighting the undead.
